req.title: System-Admin updates benefit
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a benefit through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-based benefit updates by a system-admin with mandatory field validation and audit record creation. Test[3] was flagged because a negative test (unauthenticated user gets 401) was incorrectly tagged heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is appropriate for positive state-flow tests, not for authentication-denial negative tests. The fix changes test[3] heuristic from STC to NEG, which correctly classifies it as a negative/error-pathway test aligned with the 401 unauthenticated error pathway in R-WEB-034. All other tests are preserved exactly as originally structured: (1) EP-positive for valid admin update; (2) NEG for missing mandatory field; (3) BVA for max-length boundary; (4) NEG (fixed from STC) for unauthenticated 401; (5) DT-negative for forbidden role 403.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_benefit_valid_data
preconditions: At least one benefit record exists with known id
actions: Open edit form for an existing benefit
Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
Benefit record reflects the new values in the UI
An audit record is written for the update action
trace: R-WEB-034: expected outcomes – benefit is updated; audit record written; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_admin_updates_benefit_valid_data(admin_page, existing_benefit, audit_log_client):
    page = admin_page
    benefit_id = existing_benefit["id"]
    new_name = "Updated Health Plan"
    new_description = "Comprehensive coverage"

    page.goto("/benefits")
    # open_edit_form(page, benefit_id)

    page.fill("[data-testid='benefit-name']", new_name)
    page.fill("[data-testid='benefit-description']", new_description)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/benefits" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain entity id"

    page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='success-toast']")
    assert page.is_visible("[data-testid='success-toast']")

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful update"

tests.name: test_admin_updates_benefit_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: At least one benefit record exists with known id
actions: Open edit form for an existing benefit
expected: Validation error message is displayed in the UI
No new audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-034: error pathways – 400 on validation error
R-WEB-034: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_admin_updates_benefit_missing_mandatory_field(admin_page, existing_benefit, audit_log_client):
    page = admin_page
    benefit_id = existing_benefit["id"]
    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE"))

    page.goto("/benefits")
    # open_edit_form(page, benefit_id)

    page.fill("[data-testid='benefit-name']", "")

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/benefits" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='validation-error']")
    assert page.is_visible("[data-testid='validation-error']")

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record should be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_admin_updates_benefit_name_at_max_length
preconditions: At least one benefit record exists with known id
Maximum allowed length for benefit name is 255 characters
actions: Open edit form for an existing benefit
Fill benefit-name field with exactly 255 characters
expected: Benefit record is updated with the 255-character name
trace: R-WEB-034: mandatory fields must be validated (boundary of field length)
R-WEB-034: expected outcomes – benefit is updated; response 200 or 201
tests.executable:
def test_admin_updates_benefit_name_at_max_length(admin_page, existing_benefit, audit_log_client):
    page = admin_page
    benefit_id = existing_benefit["id"]
    max_name = "B" * 255
    assert len(max_name) == 255

    page.goto("/benefits")
    # open_edit_form(page, benefit_id)

    page.fill("[data-testid='benefit-name']", max_name)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/benefits" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max-length boundary, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("name") == max_name or body.get("id") is not None

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must be written"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_benefit
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (no valid session or token)
At least one benefit record exists with known id
actions: Attempt to navigate to /benefits without authentication
Attempt to submit an update request directly to the benefit endpoint without credentials
expected: User is redirected to login page or receives an unauthorised error
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_benefit(unauthenticated_page, existing_benefit, api_client, audit_log_client):
    page = unauthenticated_page
    benefit_id = existing_benefit["id"]
    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE"))

    response = page.goto("/benefits")
    assert response.status in (401, 302, 200), "Should redirect or deny"
    if response.status == 200:
        assert page.url != "/benefits" or page.is_visible("[data-testid='login-form']")

    api_response = api_client.patch(
        f"/benefits/{benefit_id}",
        json={"name": "Should Not Update"},
        headers={}
    )
    assert api_response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {api_response.status_code}"

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_benefit
preconditions: User is authenticated with role viewer (not admin)
At least one benefit record exists with known id
actions: Authenticate as a user with role viewer
Attempt to open edit form or submit update request for an existing benefit
expected: User receives a forbidden error or the edit action is not available in the UI
trace: R-WEB-034: error pathways – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_benefit(viewer_page, existing_benefit, api_client_viewer, audit_log_client):
    page = viewer_page
    benefit_id = existing_benefit["id"]
    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE"))

    page.goto("/benefits")

    api_response = api_client_viewer.patch(
        f"/benefits/{benefit_id}",
        json={"name": "Should Not Update"}
    )
    assert api_response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {api_response.status_code}"

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=benefit_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record should be written for forbidden role attempt"
